articles.title: Fractional Reserve Banking 101
articles.summary: Banks work in ways that are unintuitive for a lot of people. articles.title: Can You Price In No Longer Pricing Things In?
articles.summary: Can You Price In No Longer Pricing Things In? By Michael Every of Rabobank At this point it isn’t a random walk but a determined march: markets have decided the Iran war and the Hormuz blockades are over, and everything is going to be better than normal imminently: the Nasdaq and S&P are at all-time highs and even worries over private credit are receding. articles.title: A New Look at Corporate Treasury Strategy
articles.summary: Published: August 2024 In the modern era of corporate finance, the balance sheet of a corporation has trended toward being less important relative to the income statement. Back during Benjamin Graham’s quintessential value investing days, he looked at balance sheet valuation metrics almost as much as he did income-related valuation metrics. articles.title: Taiwan Semi Slides Despite Record Results In Warning Sign For Chip Companies
articles.summary: Taiwan Semi Slides Despite Record Results In Warning Sign For Chip Companies Taiwanese chip giant, Taiwan Semiconductor Manfuacturing Co, said Thursday its net profit surged to a fresh record in the first quarter, fueled by the global artificial intelligence race despite the war in the Middle East. Massive demand for AI hardware means business is booming for TSMC -- the biggest contract maker of microchips used in everything from Apple phones to Nvidia processors. articles.title: QVC Shopping Channel Files Bankruptcy to Cut $5 Billion of Debt
articles.summary: Television shopping network QVC Group filed for bankruptcy Thursday as part of a plan to cut more than $5 billion of debt, as declining viewership and a shift to online retail weighed on sales and squeezed margins.

articles.title: Gold Heads for Fourth Weekly Gain on Optimism for US-Iran Truce
articles.summary: Gold headed for a fourth weekly gain after President Donald Trump expressed optimism that the US and Iran could agree a permanent ceasefire to end the war that’s upended markets and heightened inflation fears.

articles.title: Henry Paulson Suggests US Make a Break-Glass Treasuries Plan
articles.summary: Former Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson suggested US authorities prepare a back-up plan in order to avert a potential future collapse in demand for Treasuries resulting from long-running concerns over the federal debt load — an event that he warned would have “vicious” effects.

articles.title: CBP Says It Seized More Than 60 Pounds Of Cocaine From US Citizen At Border
articles.summary: CBP Says It Seized More Than 60 Pounds Of Cocaine From US Citizen At Border Authored by Troy Myers via The Epoch Times, U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) officers at the U.S.–Mexico border prevented more than 60 pounds of cocaine from entering the country, allegedly smuggled by an American citizen—a “trusted traveler”—the agency exclusively told The Epoch Times on Wednesday.
